The '''Huk War''', also known as the '''Huk Wars''' or the '''Yam'rii crisis''', was a prolonged conflict waged between the [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]] and the [[Yam'rii/Legends|Yam'rii]] of the planet [[Huk/Legends|Huk]].

==Prelude==
The conflict lasted for generations as the greedy and technologically-superior [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Yam'rii/Legends|Yam'rii]] overran neighboring [[Planet/Legends|planets]] (such as [[Abbaji (Kalee system)|Abbaji]] and [[Tovarskl]]), ruthlessly colonizing them and plundering their wealth. The insatiable Yam'rii eventually attacked nearby [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]], seeking to enslave the native Kaleesh.<ref name="US" /> They captured hundreds<ref name="TNEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> of millions<ref name="TCW CG">''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]''</ref> of Kaleesh and sold them into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]].<ref name="TNEGAS" /> Not ones to be oppressed, the Kaleesh rebelled and the two races soon became locked in violent combat. This led many Kaleesh to derogatorily refer to the Yam'rii as Huks, translating as "soulless bugs".<ref name="US" />

==The war==
[[File:Republic-arrived-at-Kalee.png|thumb|left|215px|The Galactic Republic arrived at the planet Kalee.]]
Out of this carnage arose two seemingly invincible Kaleesh [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]], the deadly [[Grievous/Legends|Qymaen jai Sheelal]] and [[Ronderu lij Kummar]]. The two fought courageously together, butchering thousands of Yam'rii in countless campaigns, their reputations raising to the level of [[God/Legends|demigods]].<ref name="US" />

Unfortunately, Ronderu lij Kummar eventually fell in battle against the Yam'rii on the beaches of Kalee, and a distraught Sheelal soon came to adopt the name "Grievous," surrounding himself with a legion of equally tortured handpicked "elite," known as the ''[[Izvoshra/Legends|Izvoshra]]'', and becoming an even more violent and rageful [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. Leading the brigades of the ''[[kolkpravis]]'' fighting force, Grievous massacred large numbers of Yam'rii in revenge, finally driving them from Kalee.<ref name="US" /> In the process, the Huk were entirely devastated, pushed back to their own world and even beyond, while Grievous, seeking retribution, co-opted Huk technology and ships for his own use, capturing all of their systems and attacking both their colonies and the Huk homeworld.<ref name="TNEGAS" />

His bloodlust and thirst for revenge did not end with the planetary retreat, and Grievous led his warriors on a campaign on the Yam'rii colony worlds, decimating both military and civilian targets. He came as far as [[Tovarskl]], when the Yam'rii, using their connections with the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], desperately petitioned the Republic for help.<ref name="US" />

The Senate inserted itself into the conflict and believed the Yam'rii's tall tales of the Kaleesh starting the conflict.<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Ruling on behalf of the Yam'rii and moving to stop the war, the Republic dispatched a Jedi task force of<ref name="US" /> fifty [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]]<ref name="LOE" /> led by Masters [[T'chooka D'oon]] and [[Jmmaar/Legends|Jmmaar]]<ref name="US" /> and—following a precedent established during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]]—paramilitary forces.<ref name="Plagueis" /> The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] leveled harsh sanctions and reparations against the Kaleesh for their aggression, ending the Huk War. Grievous, bred for battle, was forced to return to his family as the war ended.<ref name="US" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:GrievousPast-ObiWansFoe.png|thumb|right|300px|Grievous (right), veteran of the Huk War and a being with great hatred for the Jedi]]
After the war, because of the Republic's sanctions, Kalee descended into economic devastation.<ref name="US" /> In the opinion of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]], a diplomatic solution was reached to the Huk War after Republic intervention.<ref name="Plagueis" /> On the ground of Kalee, however, Grievous watched helplessly as his wives, offspring and fellow people starved, suffered and slowly died, his hatred for the Republic growing. Having little choice, Grievous became an enforcer for the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], which promised to help alleviate Kalee's plight in turn for his services. This arrangement lasted until Grievous learned that the Yam'rii went unpunished by the Republic while they desecrated Kaleesh burial grounds established on the colony worlds.<ref name="US" />

Around [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]],<ref name="Huk War restarted" /> Grievous broke his commitment to the IBC, and he and his Kaleesh elite boarded a [[Troop transport/Legends|troop shuttle]], preparing to reinitiate the Huk War. Grievous's plans were aborted, however, when his shuttle was [[Sabotage of Grievous' shuttle/Legends|destroyed]] by an [[Ion bomb/Legends|ion bomb]], secretly planted by the Banking Clan and its Separatist allies. Grievous had to be reconstructed as a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] as a means of clinging to life.<ref name="TEOR">{{StoryCite|story=The Eyes of Revolution|book=Star Wars: Visionaries}}</ref><ref name="US" /> The Kaleesh revered Grievous following his supposed death in the Huk War.<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

During the [[Yinchorri Uprising]], Valorum regarded the "Yam'rii crisis" as a recent precedent for how paramilitaries were used by the Republic to stop a problem, with a diplomatic solution following that deployment.<ref name="Plagueis" /> Though the Huk War finally seemed over, the war's legacy was touched upon during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] when Grievous returned to Tovarskl with his droid armies, and [[Battle of Tovarskl|destroyed]] the Yam'rii populace, finishing what he had started years earlier.<ref name="US" />
